evals.prompt: Create a Power Pages site for our company called 'Contoso Solutions Portal'. It should be a public-facing company portal built with React. We want to showcase our services, share company news, and let customers reach out to us.
evals.expected_output: The skill discovers the site requirements (name: Contoso Solutions Portal, framework: React, purpose: Company Portal, audience: External, location: new folder in current directory), scaffolds the React template with placeholders replaced (__SITE_NAME__ = Contoso Solutions Portal, __SITE_SLUG__ = contoso-solutions-portal), runs npm install, initializes git, starts the dev server, and shares the URL. During component planning, the user selects Bold & Vibrant aesthetic with features like Services Catalog, News/Blog, Contact Form, and Team/About section. The plan is presented and approved. Implementation applies distinctive Google Fonts, a vibrant color palette via CSS variables, motion/animations, and builds all pages with real Unsplash images. Each page and component gets its own git commit. Accessibility is verified with axe-core on every route, all critical/serious violations are fixed and re-verified. The user reviews the site via the dev server URL and is asked about deployment.
expectations: Discovery phase asks for site name, framework, purpose, audience, and project location via AskUserQuestion
Site naming values are derived: __SITE_NAME__ = 'Contoso Solutions Portal', __SITE_SLUG__ = 'contoso-solutions-portal'
React template is copied from the assets/react/ directory and all __PLACEHOLDER__ tokens are replaced
gitignore file is renamed to .gitignore in the project root
npm install is run before git init so package-lock.json is included in the initial commit
Git repository is initialized with an initial scaffold commit
Dev server is started in background via 'npm run dev' and the localhost URL is shared with the user
Playwright browser_navigate and browser_snapshot are used to verify the scaffold loads (no screenshots taken)
Component planning asks for features (context-aware options for an external company portal), aesthetic direction (Bold & Vibrant selected), and mood
Design aesthetics reference is read from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/create-site/references/design-aesthetics.md
A component plan table is presented showing pages, shared components, design elements, and routes
Color palette is determined with 6 CSS variables (--color-primary, --color-secondary, --color-bg, --color-surface, --color-text, --color-text-muted)
Implementation plan is presented with Section A (Design & Pages) and Section B (Review & Deployment) and user approval is requested
Design foundations are applied first: distinctive Google Fonts (not Inter/Roboto/Arial), CSS variables for colors, motion/animation utilities, and background treatments
The scaffold loading screen is completely replaced — no trace of Power Pages branded animation remains
Real Unsplash images are sourced via WebSearch and used in hero sections, feature cards, and content areas
Each individual page gets its own dedicated git commit (not batched together)
Each shared component gets its own dedicated git commit
Playwright browser_snapshot is used to verify each page after creation (no screenshots)
axe-core audit script is run on every route passing --url, --routes, and --project-root flags
All critical and serious accessibility violations are fixed and committed
axe-core is re-run after fixes to confirm zero critical/serious violations
An accessibility summary table is presented showing violations found vs fixed per page
User is asked to review the site at the dev server URL via AskUserQuestion
Deployment is offered via AskUserQuestion with 'Deploy now (Recommended)' and 'Skip for now' options
TaskCreate is used to create todos for all 8 phases before starting work

evals.prompt: I need an internal employee dashboard for our HR team. Call it 'PeopleHub Dashboard'. Use Vue. The team needs to see employee info, track leave requests, and post announcements. It's only for internal staff.
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies this as an internal dashboard (prioritizing data tables, functional design over flashy) with Vue framework. It scaffolds the Vue template, replaces placeholders (__SITE_NAME__ = PeopleHub Dashboard, __SITE_SLUG__ = peoplehub-dashboard), installs dependencies, initializes git, and starts the Vite dev server. During planning, Minimal & Clean aesthetic is selected with features like Employee Directory, Leave Tracker, Announcements Board, and Quick Links. Implementation applies clean typography via Google Fonts, a muted professional color palette via CSS variables, subtle motion, and builds dashboard-oriented pages with data tables and card layouts. Every page and component is individually committed. Accessibility is audited and all violations fixed. The user reviews and is asked about deployment.
expectations: Discovery identifies the site as internal-audience, which influences generation toward data tables, dashboards, and functional design
Vue template is copied from assets/vue/ directory with all placeholders replaced
Site values derived: __SITE_NAME__ = 'PeopleHub Dashboard', __SITE_SLUG__ = 'peoplehub-dashboard'
npm install runs before git init, dev server starts via 'npm run dev' in background
Dev server URL (typically localhost:5173 for Vite) is shared with the user
Scaffold is verified via Playwright browser_navigate and browser_snapshot before proceeding
Feature options are context-aware for an internal HR dashboard (e.g., Employee Directory, Leave Requests, Announcements) — not a hardcoded list
Minimal & Clean aesthetic is selected with appropriate mood pairing
Google Fonts are chosen that are distinctive (not generic Inter/Roboto/Arial) and match Minimal & Clean direction
CSS variables define a muted, professional color palette appropriate for an internal dashboard
Motion/animation plan includes subtle transitions appropriate for Minimal & Clean aesthetic
The scaffold loading screen is completely replaced with the actual dashboard UI
Dashboard-oriented pages are built with functional UI elements (tables, cards, lists) — not placeholder 'coming soon' pages
Router is updated with all new routes beyond the scaffold's default / and /about
Navigation/header component is updated with links to all pages
Individual git commits are made for each page and each shared component separately
Playwright browser_snapshot verifies each page after implementation
axe-core audit runs on every route; all critical/serious violations are fixed
Re-verification confirms zero critical/serious violations across all pages
User review is requested with the dev server URL and list of all available routes
Deployment prompt is presented — session does not end without asking about deployment

evals.prompt: Build me a landing page site called 'Aurelia Jewels' using Astro. It's for a luxury jewelry brand — we want it to feel high-end and exclusive. Target audience is external customers browsing our collections.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes this as an external-facing landing page for a luxury brand. It scaffolds the Astro template (dev server at localhost:4321), replaces placeholders (__SITE_NAME__ = Aurelia Jewels, __SITE_SLUG__ = aurelia-jewels), and launches the dev server. During planning, the user selects Elegant & Premium mood with features like Collections Showcase, Brand Story, Craftsmanship section, and Contact/Boutique Locator. Implementation applies premium typography via Google Fonts (e.g., serif display fonts), a refined color palette with gold/dark tones via CSS variables, elegant animations, and builds visually rich pages with high-quality Unsplash jewelry/luxury images. Astro-specific conventions are followed (Layout.astro for fonts, separate Header/Footer components). Each page is individually committed. Accessibility is verified and the user reviews before deployment is offered.
expectations: Discovery identifies external audience, influencing site toward landing page appeal, SEO-friendly structure, and marketing-oriented layout
Astro template is copied from assets/astro/ directory with all placeholders replaced
Site values: __SITE_NAME__ = 'Aurelia Jewels', __SITE_SLUG__ = 'aurelia-jewels'
Dev server starts at localhost:4321 (Astro default) and URL is shared with the user
Scaffold verified via Playwright before proceeding to planning
Feature options are context-aware for a luxury jewelry brand (e.g., Collections, Brand Story, Craftsmanship, Boutique Locator)
Elegant & Premium mood is selected, and design-aesthetics.md reference is consulted for the aesthetic x mood mapping
Google Fonts chosen are distinctive and appropriate for luxury branding (e.g., serif or display fonts — not Inter/Roboto/Arial)
Color palette reflects luxury/premium feel (e.g., gold, deep tones, refined neutrals) defined via CSS variables
Motion includes elegant, sophisticated animations (smooth transitions, subtle reveals) matching premium mood
Astro-specific: Layout.astro is updated to load chosen Google Fonts instead of scaffold's DM Sans + Outfit
Astro-specific: Header and Footer are separate components rewritten with proper navigation
Real Unsplash images are sourced matching luxury/jewelry aesthetic with cohesive style and lighting
Images use appropriate sizing (w=1600 for heroes, w=800 for cards) with descriptive alt text
Each page gets its own dedicated git commit; each component gets its own commit
Playwright browser_snapshot verifies each page (no screenshots taken)
axe-core audit script runs with --routes flag covering all site routes
All critical/serious accessibility violations are fixed, committed, and re-verified
Accessibility summary table shows per-page violations found vs fixed
User reviews the site and is asked for feedback via AskUserQuestion
Deployment is offered as the final step — session does not end without this prompt

evals.prompt: Create a partner hub site called 'Nexus Partner Hub' using Angular. This is for our business partners to access resources, track deals, and find partner contacts. It should feel professional and trustworthy. Put it in C:\projects\nexus-hub.
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies this as an internal partner hub with Angular framework at a specific directory. It scaffolds the Angular template (dev server at localhost:4200), replaces placeholders (__SITE_NAME__ = Nexus Partner Hub, __SITE_SLUG__ = nexus-partner-hub), and launches the dev server. During planning, Professional & Trustworthy mood is selected with features like Resource Library, Deal Tracker, Partner Directory, and Notifications/Updates. Implementation applies authoritative typography via Google Fonts, a corporate color palette via CSS variables, confident animations, and builds functional partner-oriented pages. Angular conventions are followed (styles.css for theme, Angular router). Each page and component is individually committed. Accessibility is verified on all routes and the user reviews before deployment is offered.
expectations: Discovery resolves project location to the user-specified path C:\projects\nexus-hub instead of defaulting to current directory
Angular template is copied from assets/angular/ directory with all placeholders replaced
Site values: __SITE_NAME__ = 'Nexus Partner Hub', __SITE_SLUG__ = 'nexus-partner-hub'
Dev server starts at localhost:4200 (Angular default) and URL is shared with the user
Scaffold verified via Playwright browser_navigate and browser_snapshot
Internal audience is identified, prioritizing functional navigation depth and data-oriented layouts
Feature options are context-aware for a partner hub (e.g., Resource Library, Deal Tracker, Partner Directory, Notifications)
Professional & Trustworthy mood is selected and mapped to design choices via the aesthetics reference
Google Fonts chosen convey authority and professionalism (not generic Inter/Roboto/Arial)
Angular-specific: styles.css (not theme.css) is used for design tokens and CSS variables
Angular-specific: Angular router is configured with all new routes
Color palette uses corporate, trustworthy tones defined via 6 CSS variables
Motion/animations are confident and restrained, matching Professional & Trustworthy mood
TodoList is created upfront with all 8 phases tracked via TaskCreate before any work begins
Each individual page and component gets its own dedicated git commit
Playwright browser_snapshot verifies each page after creation
axe-core audit runs on every route; violations are fixed and re-verified until zero critical/serious issues
Review phase presents a summary table of pages, components, design elements, and git commits
Dev server URL and all available routes are shared with user for review
User is asked for feedback via AskUserQuestion during review phase
Deployment is offered with 'Deploy now (Recommended)' and 'Skip for now' options
Skill usage is recorded via skill tracking reference before deployment prompt
